turns.user_message: I just closed my laptop for the third time today and realized I hadn't actually finished a single thing. The screen went dark and I just sat there listening to the hum of the room, completely drained. It feels like every little task is suddenly a mountain I don't have the energy to climb. I keep telling myself I'll just push through it tomorrow, but honestly, my head is just full of static right now.

That same quiet moment after I finally shut everything down has been happening almost every single afternoon lately. I used to bounce back after a rough stretch, but now the fatigue just lingers and seeps into everything else I try to do. I catch myself staring at the same blank wall, wondering why my usual drive has completely vanished. It’s like a heavy fog that refuses to lift, no matter how much I try to shake it off.

I keep returning to that exact same quiet moment when the screen finally goes black, but now it feels like a daily surrender instead of just a pause. The exhaustion has completely hollowed me out, leaving me completely numb to deadlines and expectations that used to actually matter to me. I sit there in the dark and feel absolutely nothing but a heavy, dragging weight in my chest that won't budge. Every single morning I wake up dreading that familiar click, knowing I have nothing left to give.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens by referencing a specific recent moment.
